family.name: Wood_Storage_Cabinet_Image_Test_001
family.description: Image-derived freestanding wood storage cabinet with left doors, right drawers, handles, frame trim, and legs.

familyStrategy.categoryReason: Furniture is appropriate because the object is freestanding movable storage content.
familyStrategy.hostingReason: Non-hosted because the cabinet does not cut or depend on a wall, floor, ceiling, roof, or face host.
familyStrategy.loadableFamilyReason: This is reusable furniture content that may be loaded, placed, scheduled, and reused across projects.
familyStrategy.scheduleTagNeeds: Overall dimensions, finish/materials, and type name should be schedule-ready in a future builder pass.
familyStrategy.renderingNeeds: Wood finish and brass hardware should be material-controlled; detailed wood grain is out of scope for v0.1.

referencePlaneStrategy.name: Left/Right
referencePlaneStrategy.intent: Control overall width and side panel locations.
referencePlaneStrategy.drives: Left and right side panels, front frame, drawer bay, door bay.

referencePlaneStrategy.name: Front/Back
referencePlaneStrategy.intent: Control overall depth, front face frame, recessed panel plane, and back panel.
referencePlaneStrategy.drives: Case panels, door/drawer setbacks, hardware offsets.

referencePlaneStrategy.name: Bottom/Top
referencePlaneStrategy.intent: Control overall height, leg zone, body zone, and top panel.

referencePlaneStrategy.name: Door Drawer Split
referencePlaneStrategy.intent: Control the vertical divider between the door bay and drawer stack.
referencePlaneStrategy.drives: Central divider, left doors, right drawer fronts.

referencePlaneStrategy.name: Hardware Centerlines
referencePlaneStrategy.intent: Future builder should place pull nested families or sweeps from named centerline planes.

parameterStrategy.name: Width
parameterStrategy.drives: Overall left/right reference planes and dependent panels.

parameterStrategy.name: Depth
parameterStrategy.drives: Overall front/back reference planes and panel depths.

parameterStrategy.name: Panel Thickness
parameterStrategy.intent: Type parameter for side, top, bottom, back, door, drawer, and face-frame thickness logic.
parameterStrategy.notes: Current recipe uses fixed values until builder supports formulas and locks.

parameterStrategy.name: Reveal Gap
parameterStrategy.intent: Type parameter controlling minimal gaps between doors, drawers, and face frame.
parameterStrategy.notes: Target default should be 1/8 in to 1/4 in.

parameterStrategy.name: Hardware Offset
parameterStrategy.intent: Type parameter controlling pull placement from panel edges or centerlines.

nestedFamilies.name: Cabinet_Bar_Pull_With_Returns
nestedFamilies.purpose: Reusable pull family or sweep-based nested family with end returns back to the door/drawer face.
nestedFamilies.notes: Current recipe approximates pulls with rectangular extrusions.

nestedFamilies.name: Tapered_Round_Cabinet_Leg
nestedFamilies.purpose: Reusable tapered blend leg family placed from leg centerline reference planes.
nestedFamilies.notes: Current recipe approximates legs with rectangular extrusions.

visibilityStrategy.coarse: Simplified rectangular cabinet outline and leg massing.
visibilityStrategy.medium: Panelized body, doors, drawers, frame, pulls, and legs.
visibilityStrategy.fine: Future sweep/blend details, bevels, pull returns, and refined reveals.
visibilityStrategy.planRcp: Future plan symbol or simplified top projection should avoid excessive hardware detail.

publishingQa: Flex width, depth, and height after reference-plane and parameter support is implemented.
Verify pulls are nested or sweep-based before production use.
Verify legs are tapered blends before production use.
Verify no unassociated geometry remains before library publishing.
Verify materials are exposed as user-facing material parameters.

geometry.name: Left Side Panel
geometry.notes: Panelized cabinet carcass replaces the earlier single solid body.

geometry.name: Right Side Panel
geometry.notes: Right side panel.

geometry.name: Top Panel
geometry.notes: Top case panel.

geometry.name: Bottom Panel
geometry.notes: Bottom case panel above legs.

geometry.name: Back Panel
geometry.notes: Thin back panel keeps the family from being one solid mass.

geometry.name: Left Door A
geometry.notes: Approximate left front door recessed into the face frame.
geometry.intent: Door panel should be recessed inside the internal faceplate with a minimal 1/8 in to 1/4 in reveal.

geometry.name: Left Door B
geometry.notes: Approximate right leaf of left door bay recessed into the face frame.
geometry.intent: Door panel should be recessed inside the internal faceplate with a minimal 1/8 in to 1/4 in reveal.

geometry.name: Top Drawer Front
geometry.notes: Top drawer front approximated as a flat recessed panel.
geometry.intent: Drawer front should sit slightly back from the face frame with minimal reveals between adjacent drawer panels.

geometry.name: Second Drawer Front
geometry.notes: Second drawer front approximated as a flat recessed panel.
geometry.intent: Drawer front should sit slightly back from the face frame with minimal reveals between adjacent drawer panels.

geometry.name: Third Drawer Front
geometry.notes: Third drawer front approximated as a flat recessed panel.
geometry.intent: Drawer front should sit slightly back from the face frame with minimal reveals between adjacent drawer panels.

geometry.name: Bottom Drawer Front
geometry.notes: Bottom drawer front approximated as a flat recessed panel.
geometry.intent: Drawer front should sit slightly back from the face frame with minimal reveals between adjacent drawer panels.

geometry.name: Top Front Rail
geometry.notes: Approximates thick top front frame.
geometry.intent: Front frame should eventually be a sweep with a profiled bevel sloping toward the cabinet center.
geometry.approximationReason: Current builder approximates the profiled front rail as a rectangular extrusion until sweep/profile support exists.

geometry.name: Bottom Front Rail
geometry.notes: Approximates lower front rail above legs.
geometry.intent: Front frame should eventually be a sweep with a profiled bevel sloping toward the cabinet center.
geometry.approximationReason: Current builder approximates the profiled front rail as a rectangular extrusion until sweep/profile support exists.

geometry.name: Left Front Stile
geometry.notes: Left vertical frame.
geometry.intent: Face frame stile should eventually be a sweep/profile condition rather than a plain rectangular extrusion.
geometry.approximationReason: Current builder approximates the profiled stile as a rectangular extrusion until sweep/profile support exists.

geometry.name: Middle Front Stile
geometry.notes: Divider between door bay and drawer bay.
geometry.intent: Central divider should be modeled as an internal dividing panel plus a slightly set-back face panel.

geometry.name: Right Front Stile
geometry.notes: Right vertical frame.
geometry.intent: Face frame stile should eventually be a sweep/profile condition rather than a plain rectangular extrusion.
geometry.approximationReason: Current builder approximates the profiled stile as a rectangular extrusion until sweep/profile support exists.

geometry.name: Left Door Vertical Handle
geometry.notes: Slim vertical pull matching the source image.
geometry.intent: Pull should eventually be modeled as a sweep path with small end returns back to the door face.
geometry.approximationReason: Current builder approximates pull hardware as rectangular extrusion geometry until nested sweep hardware is supported.

geometry.name: Front Left Leg
geometry.notes: Rectangular placeholder for tapered angled leg.
geometry.intent: Leg should eventually be modeled as a tapered round blend.
geometry.approximationReason: Current builder approximates tapered round legs as rectangular extrusions until blend support exists.

geometry.approximationReason: Current builder approximates tapered round legs as rectangular extrusions until blend support exists.

assumptions: Overall product dimensions were provided as 42 in wide, 18 in deep, and 45 in high, then converted to millimeters.
Leg height, body height, frame thickness, door split, and front component locations are inferred from the image.
Wood grain, bevels, drawer gaps, and angled/tapered legs are simplified; the body is now panelized and handles remain rectangular to match the source image.
The builder does not yet create flexing Revit parameters or constraints from the recipe.
clarifyingQuestions: Should the left bay be one large door, two doors, or sliding panels?
Should drawer fronts be separated by real reveals/gaps or only visual front panels?
Should legs be angled/tapered solids once the builder supports tapered geometry?

warnings: Overall dimensions are provided, but internal component dimensions are inferred from visual interpretation.
Family should be rebuilt after internal component dimensions are confirmed.
